checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=7,760,000 (end 2014-12-31) vs 4,946,132 (end 2013-12-31), a 57% increase, while Revenues=63,548,349 (FY2014) vs 68,169,623 (FY2013), a 6.8% decline
evidence.location: 10-Q filed 2015-11-05, balance sheet AccountsReceivableNetCurrent; 10-K filed 2015-03-17, Revenues

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=14,690,994 (2015-03-31) vs 7,760,000 (2014-12-31), an 89% quarterly jump, while Revenues=20,816,276 (Q1 2015) vs 16,393,000 (Q4 2014), a 27% quarterly increase
evidence.location: 10-Q filed 2015-05-08, balance sheet and income statement

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent fell to 10,321,000 (2015-09-30) from 14,419,602 (2015-06-30) even as Revenues remained strong at 19,814,000 (Q3 2015) vs 21,457,267 (Q2 2015)

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=8,170,214 (FY2014) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivitiesContinuingOperations=18,401,298 (FY2014) -- cash flow exceeded income in FY2013-14, but reversed sharply in Q1 2015 coincident with the AR spike

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: Gross margin implied by Revenues=68,169,623 and CostOfGoodsSold=22,481,122 (FY2013) is ~67%, but Revenues=63,548,349 and CostOfGoodsSold=28,022,034 (FY2014) implies gross margin ~56%, a large compression coincident with a revenue decline

InventoryNet=15,899,000 (2015-09-30) up from InventoryNet=13,341,783 (2014-09-30), a ~19% rise, while quarterly revenue grew similarly (Revenues=19,814,000 Q3 2015 vs 15,278,000 Q3 2014, ~30%) -- broadly consistent but margin trend remains a concern

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?
Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent held flat at 10,000 from 2011-03-31 through 2014-09-30 despite AccountsReceivableNetCurrent growing from 1,259,607 (2011-03-31) to 7,052,908 (2014-09-30)
Allowance then jumped to 41,000 (2014-12-31) and 73,550 (2015-06-30) coincident with the AR spike, but remained well under 1% of gross receivables throughout

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: NT 10-K filed 2015-03-03, followed by 10-K filed 2015-03-17 (14 days after due date)
A cluster of SEC CORRESP/UPLOAD filings (2014-12-23, 2014-12-30, 2015-01-20, 2015-02-13, 2015-02-19, 2015-02-25) immediately precedes the NT 10-K and delayed 10-K

Earlier extensive SEC correspondence clusters also occurred in 2007 (UPLOAD/CORRESP March-May 2007) and 2009 (CORRESP/UPLOAD March-September 2009), suggesting recurring periods of SEC accounting review

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data provided spans fiscal periods from 2009-04-01 through 2015-09-30 across Revenues, AccountsReceivableNetCurrent, InventoryNet, NetIncomeLoss, and cash flow line items, filed across multiple 10-K and 10-Q accessions (e.g., 0001193125-12-116662, 0001193125-15-095569, 0001193125-15-368180)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Possible premature or front-loaded revenue recognition (e.g., large license/collaboration invoices or extended customer payment terms) causing receivables to balloon disproportionately to revenue, followed by a subsequent reversal/collection catch-up (AR fell from 14.4M in Q2 2015 to 10.3M in Q3 2015) and a negative operating cash flow quarter despite positive net income in Q1 2015.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=14,690,994 (2015-03-31) vs 7,760,000 (2014-12-31), an 89% quarterly increase, while Revenues rose only ~27% quarter-over-quarter
AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew 57% (4,946,132 to 7,760,000) in FY2014 while Revenues declined 6.8% (68,169,623 to 63,548,349)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Purchase accounting from a mid-2014 acquisition materially increased goodwill and intangibles (from under $1M/~$6M respectively to over $13M/$16M) while gross margin compressed sharply from ~67% (FY2013) to ~56% (FY2014); if acquired inventory/backlog was written up and amortized irregularly, or if impairment testing assumptions were optimistic, goodwill/intangibles could be overstated relative to the acquired business's actual cash-generating capacity.

rationale_evidence.quote: Gross margin fell from ~67% (Revenues=68,169,623, CostOfGoodsSold=22,481,122, FY2013) to ~56% (Revenues=63,548,349, CostOfGoodsSold=28,022,034, FY2014)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Allowance for doubtful accounts was held nearly static at $10,000 for years despite receivables growing several-fold, suggesting the reserve may not have kept pace with credit risk, potentially overstating net receivables during the high-growth AR periods before the allowance was later increased.

rationale_evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=10,000 unchanged from 2011-03-31 through 2014-09-30 while AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew from 1,259,607 to 7,052,908 over the same span

top_signals: Accounts receivable grew far faster than revenue in multiple periods (57% AR growth vs -6.8% revenue in FY2014; 89% AR growth vs 27% revenue growth in Q1 2015), followed by an AR pullback in Q3 2015
SEC CORRESP/UPLOAD correspondence cluster (Dec 2014-Feb 2015) immediately preceded an NT 10-K and a late-filed 10-K for FY2014, suggesting possible accounting scrutiny

Gross margin compressed sharply from ~67% (FY2013) to ~56% (FY2014) coincident with a large goodwill/intangibles increase from a mid-2014 acquisition, while allowance for doubtful accounts remained minimal relative to a growing receivables base
